title: Technical Officer (Field Operations)
description:
Responsibilities:

  • Carry out all field operation activities, including isolation & normalisation, in line with operational guidelines
  • Identifying and reporting on defective plant and equipment
  • Monitor and log equipment operation data in to associated logs
  • Responding appropriately to system exigencies as required
  • Carrying out all routine plant tests and periodic auxiliary equipment changeover in accordance with approved Work Instructions and schedules.
  • Assist CCR staff in maintaining accurate, up to date and timely records of all key operational events
  • Assist in monitoring performance and maintaining optimum unit efficiency in operations
  • Investigate plant abnormalities and recommend course of action during shift (plant trips, unit de-ration, occurrence of fire etc.)
  • Preparing generating units activities to come on stream in accordance with real time dispatch schedule
  • Carry out first level maintenance if required on instructions of Shift Leader
  • Monitor plant condition (first level condition monitoring)
  • Co-ordinate OJT training for new team members

Requirements:

  • Preferably Diploma or technical certification in Mechanical/Electrical Engineering, or equivalent qualifications. Higher NITEC candidates may also be considered if equivalent, extensive related experience can be demonstrated.
  • Diploma holder with 2-5 years’ experience in Combined Cycle Power Plants or related industries
  • Higher NITEC with 7-10 years’ experience in Combined Cycle Power Plants or related industries.
  • Self-motivated, independent, possess sound analytical and communication skills, and is a team player.
  • Possesses good knowledge of plant processes and operations.
  • Must not be suffering from colour blindness due to work requirement to handle equipment or machinery.

Location and working hours:

  • Jurong Island in Singapore
  • Must be able to work on 12-hour rotating shifts. (10am to 10pm and/or 10 pm to 10am)

Interested applicants, please forward your detailed resume with enclosed photograph including your last drawn salary, expected salary and availability to HR.SG@pacificlight.com.sg

Please indicate the position that you are applying on the subject.

(All applications will be treated in strictest confidence. We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be notified)

By submitting your personal data and/or resume to PacificLight (“us”) or to HR.SG@pacificlight.com.sg, you shall be deemed to have given consent to us collecting, using, and disclosing your personal data for the purpose of assessing your job application. Information collected may also be disclosed to PacificLight Group of Companies (“PacificLight”) which includes PacificLight Power Pte. Ltd and PacificLight Energy Pte. Ltd. for this purpose. Please read our privacy policy here“ to promote easy accessibility.

postedCompany.description: PacificLight Power Pte Ltd is a Singapore based integrated energy company that strives to be a one-stop energy partner for businesses. It is a highly valued investment by two international corporations – FPM Power Holdings and PETRONAS Power Sdn Bhd. Our power plant, which houses a state-of-the-art 800 MW natural gas fired combined cycle facility has been supplying electricity via our subsidiary company, PacificLight Energy Pte Ltd, to Singapore consumers since 2013. We are looking for candidates who are committed, talented and possess the needed passion to be successful in his/her job to join our team of experienced professionals. PacificLight strongly believes in developing our people through training and a performance-based reward system
postedCompany.name: PACIFICLIGHT POWER PTE. LTD.
